title: Correcting GIT Cherry-Picked Commit Hash Validation for Non-ASCII Characters

statement.markdown: Implement a function `is_valid_cherry_pick_hash` that takes a string representing a commit hash as input and returns `True` if it is a valid cherry-picked commit hash, and `False` otherwise. A valid commit hash is one that consists of 40 hexadecimal digits and is at least 40 characters long. For this task, assume that the input commit hash will only contain English alphabet characters and non-ASCII characters. However, the function must correctly handle commit hashes with non-ASCII characters, such as those containing Unicode characters like accents, diacritical marks, or emojis.
statement.inputFormat: A single string representing a commit hash.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the commit hash is valid.
statement.notes: Note that a valid commit hash must be at least 40 characters long and consist of 40 hexadecimal digits. The function should correctly handle commit hashes with non-ASCII characters.

constraints.description: The length of the commit hash is between 1 and 10000 characters.

examples.explanation: This commit hash is valid because it consists of 40 hexadecimal digits and is at least 40 characters long.
This commit hash is invalid because it is less than 40 characters long.
This commit hash is invalid because it is less than 40 characters long.
This commit hash is valid because it consists of 40 hexadecimal digits and is at least 40 characters long.

hints.text: First hint text: Remember to check the length of the commit hash and ensure it consists of only hexadecimal digits.
